publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-11-02 18:04:56 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, Diaries, Printed Materials, Publications, Audio Recordings.
did.abstract: Letters, diaries, deeds and other papers of the related Shanklin and Avery families.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Letters, diaries, deeds and other papers of the related Shanklin and Avery families; diary of John Gilbert Shanklin of Evansville, Indiana, in which he wrote of people and places observed on a summer trip through the northwestern United States, 1863; deeds, appraisals, indentures and other legal papers, 1828-1873, mostly involving land in Evansville; cancelled checks, receipts, bills, 1828-1868; also, playbills, programs, clippings, and miscellaneous items; taped interview with transcript of Malvina Harlan Shanklin, 1959, in which she provides reminiscences of her early life.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Shanklin and Avery family papers, #1635. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a copy of the finding aid. (PDF file)

did.unittitle: Series I. Correspondence and legal papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Jane Perry (?) to Mr. Shanklin, Carrickmagra

did.unittitle: F. E. Walker to Isaac T. Preston

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from F. E. Walker, Henderson, to Isaac T. Preston, New Orleans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Charles Diamond, New Orleans, to John Shanklin, Evansville, Indiana

did.unittitle: Susan H. Cheshire to Benjamin F. Avery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters to Benjamin F. Avery, Louisville, Kentucky from his wife Susan H. Cheshire

did.unittitle: Benjamin Avery to Susan H. Cheshire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Benjamin F. Avery to his wife Susan H. Cheshire, Jackson, Mississippi to Louisville, Kentucky.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Susan Cheshire to Benjamin, Louisville to Meadsville

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from J. P. Walworth, Natches to Benjamin Avery

did.unittitle: Marias (?) to Mrs. P. H. Avery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Marias (?) to Mrs. P. H. Avery, Hartford

did.unittitle: J. G. Shanklin to James M. Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from J. G. Shanklin, Evansville, to James M. Shanklin, Crawfordsville, Indiana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from George Shanklin from brother Gilbert Shanklin, Geneva, Switzerland. Describing Paris and discussing Fr. Lavoisier.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to George from Gilbert, Carrickmagra. Criticizes party platform

did.unittitle: G. W. Shanklin to Francis W. Miller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from G. W. Shanklin as attorney for John Shanklin to Francis W. Miller, Evansville to Cincinnati

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from B. F. Avery to Gertie, Louisville

did.unittitle: Samuel R. Dowery to J. G. Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Samuel R. Dowery (Private Secretary to Gov.), Indianapolis to J. G. Shanklin, Wyoming, N.Y.

did.unittitle: Susan H. Avery to Mrs. Reilly

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Susan H. Avery to Mrs. Reilly, Wyoming, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Daniel Macauley to Gil, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Daniel Macauley to John Black, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Daniel Macauley to Shanklin, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Daniel Macauley to Gil Shanklin, Washington, D.C.

did.unittitle: G. W. Shanklin to Mr. Wilson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from G. W. Shanklin to Mr. Wilson, Evansville, Indiana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Edwin Markham to Mr. Brown, West New Brighton, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from B. F. Avery in letter to wife, wrote a copy of his letter to D. H., a relative, dissolving a partnership.

did.unittitle: E. W. A. to brother and sister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from E. W. A. [Arms] (?) to his brother and sister. Tells of illnesses: cholera, "hysterics", consumption. Speaks of whirlwind in town due to California gold rush. General news and gossip.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Benjamin F. Avery to Susan (?) on board the "Southern Belle" by Ground Gulf, (Louisiana?). Tells of business transaction made and anxiety to return home to see his children.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Gill to his father from Frankfort, Kentucky (?). Explained amount of money needed for school there and how it would be used. Description of a debate held near Frankfort. Soon to return home.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Southfield, Redfind, near Detroit from Gill to his parents during his travels thru Ohio, Michigan. Describes the warm hospitality. Discusses fully his views on the Civil War - friendly negotiations, realize plight of south if emancipation took place. Use of the "olive branch" (His diary tells of travels) see p. 5 of this inventory.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Edie from her cousin Gill from Aughrygalt (Ireland?) Europe. Philosophizes and tells of accident - sprained ankle and experience with Perry family, with whom he lives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Gilbert Shanklin to his sister Lizzie Shanklin from Strunorear describing Bologna - its streets, palaces, people and also Venice - its palaces, people and advances. Original (poor condition) and 2 typed copies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Gil Shanklin to Edie wishing her a Merry Christmas and Happy New Year.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Edie from cousin Gil from Braunschweig. Tells of his regard for German people and their language. Mentions characteristics of other nationalities - French, English, Italian.

did.unittitle: Benjamin Laforce McDougall to Miss Gatty

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Benjamin Laforce McDougall to Miss Gatty expressing sadness to be leaving his seat at her desk because of the Board's decision to change the meeting place. (sketches)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Gil to Edie Shanklin from Berlin. Departure from Braunschweig sad. Start in at University in Berlin soon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Gil to his sister from Evansville, Indiana. News and family talk.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Joaquin Miller to Mrs. Avery from New York City. Personal note.

did.unittitle: Joaquin Miller to Mrs. Gertrude A. Avery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Joaquin Miller to Mrs. Gertrude Avery from New York City. Personal note.

did.unittitle: Vinnie Ream Roxie to John G. Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vinnie Ream Roxie sends a congratulatory wedding note to Hon. John G. Shanklin - from Washington, D.C.

did.unittitle: John W. Foster to John G. Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John W. Foster sends a congratulatory wedding note to Hon. John G. Shanklin - sent from Mexico.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dan MacCauley's of Boston writes Gil Shanklin a personal note recalling old days gone by. Mentions the Shanklin baby girl.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gill Shanklin writes to his old friend from New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Dan MacCauley again writes to his old friend Gill Shanklin from New York City.

did.unittitle: Mary L. Coonley to Gill Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Mary L. Coonley writes to her Uncle Gill Shanklin from Hillside (school?) Thanks him for a newspaper he sent her.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marie Prescott (actress) writes thank you note to Editor of the Evansville, Indiana paper, for a prized criticism. From Buffalo, N.Y. (The Courier, of which JGS was editor)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marie Prescott writes to Evansville's newspaper critic to give good notices of her Co. due to upset within the players. They are coming to Evansville soon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mallie Shanklin writes her pa-pa (Gill) from Hillside.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gill Shanklin writes to his daughter Malvina from Evansville.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gill Shanklin writes to daughter, Mallie from Hot Springs, Arkansas where he went for treatments on his eyes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Mallie from her grandmother for her birthday wish.

did.unittitle: D. W. Voorhees to John G. Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Hon. John G. Shanklin from D. W. Voorhees from Asheville, N.C. thanking him for his account of the Scottsburg meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna and Malvina Shanklin from their mother in Anchorage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna and Malvina Shanklin from their mother in Anchorage telling date she will return home.

did.unittitle: Pendennis Club to C. B. R.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: C.B.R. (?) from Pendennis Club in Louisville, Kentucky thought it would be nice to send to Gil the seconds who backed his name for membership - somehow Gil Shanklin was sent the letter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Susanna and Malvina receive a letter from their mother from Indianapolis, Indiana. She and her husband are at a convention.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gill Shanklin writes to Mallie at Hillside from Evansville.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mallie received a note from her Grandmother.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of seconds from the Pendennis Club, as mentioned on April 4, 1892

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mallie, in Chicago, receives a letter from her father in Evansville. His eyes are poorer. The girls and their mother are attending the World's Fair.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from M. H. Ingrim, proprietor of the Winamac Democrat Journal congratulating him on his coming appointment as Consul to Berlin, Germany or Manchester, England.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mallie's uncle writes to her from Evansville.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Susanna, in Chicago, writes to her friend and tells her of the fun she is having at the Fair.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Mallie at Hillside from her Father at Evansville.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Mallie from her cousin, Helen Avery Robinson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Mallie from her cousin, Helen Avery Robinson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Mallie from her cousin, Helen Avery Robinson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Mallie from her cousin, Helen Avery Robinson.

did.unittitle: John W. Foster to John G. Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from John W. Foster in Washington, D. C. writes Hon. John Gilbert Shanklin to thank him for sending him the Evansville Courier file of notices of deaths.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Mallie from her cousin, Helen Avery Robinson.

did.unittitle: W. T. Price to Gil Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: W. T. Price wrote letter of sympathy to Gil Shanklin upon the death of his brother, George.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna from her father, in Evansville, Indiana dictated to Mallie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna from her grandmother (and whom she was named after), Susanna in Evansville and grandmother in Anchorage, Kentucky

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna from Mallie, speaks of weddings, gossip, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna S. from Mallie, miscellaneous topics - mentions children.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna S. from her father, miscellaneous topics, gossip.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Mallie S. from her mother. Mallie is traveling with the Bingham family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Mallie S. from her mother from Anchorage, Kentucky.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna S. from her father dictated to A. P. (?) from Evansville, Indiana. General news.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna S. from her father dictated to A. P. from Evansville, Indiana. General news.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna S. from her father dictated to A. P. from Evansville, Indiana. General news.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna S. from her father dictated to A. P. from Evansville, Indiana. General news, mentions affliction Susanna had.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Gertrude (Avery?) from her sister Lydia in Rochester. Lydia is loaning Mallie money to study French and take singing lessons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Gertrude and the girls (Mallie and Susanna?) from their mother from Hillside.

did.unittitle: Grandmother Avery to Susanna, Mallie, Goldie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna, Mallie, & Goldie from Grandmother Avery from Hillside.

did.unittitle: S. M. MacDonald to J. W. Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to J.W. Shanklin from S. M. MacDonald from Wyoming, N.Y. (Hillside) report on Mrs. Avery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Bon (?), Hillside Farm, N.Y. to his sister.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna S. from cousin Edith Reilly concerning some early clippings of her father's activities which she sent Susanna.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Mallie from Willard Hubbell of Coral Gables, Florida discussing list prices of portrait done by Henry Sales Hubbell. Printed list price accompanies this.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Uncle George from Malvina H. Shanklin sometime in 1893. Written from 'Chicago, Illinois.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Gertrude from her sister Lydia from Rochester.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Gertrude from her sister Lydia discussing the loan of money to Mallie for singing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Gertrude from her sister Lydia again discussing Mallie and her singing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Mallie from her Grandmother.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna from her Father.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Susanna S. from her Father dictated to Mallie. Note from Mallie on the end. Susanna is in Virginia teaching school.

did.unittitle: J. W. Raymond to Dr. Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from J. W. Raymond (?) to Dr. Shanklin from Louisville, Kentucky - declines invitation because of previous engagement.

did.unittitle: S. M. M. (?) to J. W. Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Denison, Erwin Hotel Co. Proprietors, Indianapolis, Indiana - addressed to Miss Shanklin, 620 Locust Street, Evansville, Indiana.

did.unittitle: St. George Hotel to Critic of Courier

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: St. George Hotel, Electric Light in Every Room - addressed to Critic of Courier

did.unittitle: Hotel Hay to Malvina Harlan Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hotel Hay, Hot Springs, Arkansas - addressed to Miss Malvina Harlan Shanklin, Louisville, Kentucky.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Playbills transferred to Broadside Collection 6/61

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Margaret Mather, "The Honeymoon" - poor condition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kate Castleton, "A Paper Doll"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mrs. D. P. Bowers, "Lady Audley's Secret"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: W. J. Scanlan, "Shane Na Lawn"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marie Prescott, "Czeka" - poor condition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marie Prescott, "Ingomar"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miss Minnie Maddern, "Caprice"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: McCoull Opera Comique Co., "The Black Hussar"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: McCoull Opera Comique Co., "Falka"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lotta, "The Little Detective"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: N. Y. Original Cast, "Held By the Enemy"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mr. & Mrs. W. J. Florence, "Our Governor"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marie Prescott, "Czeka"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bennett and Moulton's Comic Opera Co., "Bohemian Girl"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bennett and Moulton's Comic Opera Co., "Chimes of Normandy" - poor condition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet of operas put on by the National Opera Co., Exposition Music Hall in Louisville, Kentucky

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The New American Opera Co., at the Opera House in Evansville, Indiana, producing "Troubadour"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gilmore and His Incomparable Bank of N.Y. - In Evans Hall, Evansville, Indiana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American Opera Program. Southern exposition Building. 2 copies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Oregon Country in Early Days" from unknown newspaper. Concerns poems of Joaquin Miller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to the Editor from G. A. Shanklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Wise Sayings From Mrs. Avery's Note Book"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Courier, Evansville, Indiana. Obituary of George W. Shanklin

did.unittitle: Clipping of newspaper concerning Gil Shanklin in politics
Poem which accompanied a Christmas gift from Waldo (?) to his mother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chronicle of journey; describing attitude of people in Ohio, Kentucky, Indiana, New York, and Pennsylvania. Written by Gill, with many observations on people and places, Cincinnati, Detroit, Niagara Falls, Montreal, Boston, the Hudson River Valley, New York City, and Pittsburgh. Handwritten in pencil.

did.unittitle: Notes and Extracts about Money, Banks, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Started July 1902, 11 pages handwritten quotes and book lists (leather bound notebook).

did.unittitle: Dinner to Mr. Justice John Marshall Harlan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed 86 page book containing speeches at dinner given in his honor by the Bar of the Supreme Court of the United States on December 9, 1902
